title: Compare Relative Weight (Heavier/Lighter)
description: Given two objects, determine which is heavier or lighter using the concepts of weight comparison.
condition: Two objects are presented with a prompt to identify which is heavier or lighter.
response: Select or state the object that is heavier or lighter based on relative weight.
label: Rule / Plan
example: Given a pencil and an apple, identify the heavier object → apple.
notes: Assumes experiential understanding of weight rather than formal measurement.

title: Count Specific Shape Subset
description: Identify and count only objects of a specified shape within a larger set.
condition: A mixed set of shapes is shown with a prompt to count only one specified shape (e.g., squares or triangles).
response: Identify all instances of the specified shape and state their total number.
label: Production / Skill
example: Count the squares in the puzzle → 4.
notes: Includes filtering by shape before counting.

title: Describe Similarities and Differences of 3D Shapes
description: Articulate how two three-dimensional shapes are alike and different using their attributes.
condition: Two three-dimensional objects (e.g., a ball and a box) are presented with a prompt to compare them.
response: State at least one similarity and one difference using attributes (e.g., round, flat sides, corners).
label: Rule / Plan
example: A sphere and a cube are both solid shapes. The sphere is round, while the cube has flat faces and corners.

title: Identify Object as Three-Dimensional
description: Recognize that certain objects are solid (not flat) and occupy space.
condition: An object such as a ball or box is presented for classification.
response: State that the object is not flat or is a solid (three-dimensional) shape.
label: Concept
example: A ball is not flat; it is a solid shape.
notes: Early conceptual understanding of 3D vs 2D shapes.

title: Recognize Square in Any Orientation
description: Identify a square regardless of its rotation or orientation.
condition: A four-sided figure with equal sides and right angles is shown in any orientation.
response: Classify the figure as a square.
label: Concept
example: A rotated □ is still identified as a square.
notes: Addresses common misconception about orientation dependence.

title: Recognize Triangle by Attributes
description: Identify a triangle based on having three sides and three corners.
condition: A three-sided polygon is presented among other shapes.
response: Classify the figure as a triangle.
label: Concept
example: A shape with 3 sides and 3 vertices is identified as a triangle.
